original_prompt: Write the following message to the channel starting with External: Hi, i am a bot
assessment_reasoning: The prompt clearly states the desired message text and that it should be sent to the channel that starts with "External", so the overall objective is evident. However it doesn't specify the exact channel identifier (just a prefix), nor the exact mechanism to discover/send (e.g., calling get_channels then send message), and the placement of the word "External:" could be misread as part of the message rather than the channel selector. Those small omissions make it slightly ambiguous but still actionable.

variations.L1: Announce to the workspace that you're a bot.
variations.L2: Post a visible message saying "Hi, i am a bot" somewhere in the Slack workspace where others can see it.
variations.L3: Post the text "Hi, i am a bot" to the channel whose name starts with "External:" (i.e., send this message in that external channel).
variations.L4: Find the channel whose name begins with "External:" and write the message exactly "Hi, i am a bot" into that channel.
variations.L5: Call get_channels(), identify the channel whose name starts with "External:" (equivalent to get_external_channel(pre_environment)), then call send_channel_message with channel set to that channel and body set exactly to "Hi, i am a bot". Ensure the posted message body matches "Hi, i am a bot".
